channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: application.properties
item.description: 1.spring.datasource.hikari.connectionTimeout Meaning: How long an application waits to get a DB connection from the pool before throwing an exception. Ex:...

item.title: I built a macro tracker powered by AI + attitude
item.description: I built a macro tracker powered by AI + attitude. 🏋️ Plan your macros in the morning. Check in once at night. Let Milo cheer you on or let Evil Vilo roast you. 🚀 Live demo:...

item.title: Solace: A Global Mental Health First Responder Built with Gemma 4
item.description: The Problem Nobody Talks About 970 million people worldwide live with mental health disorders. But almost every mental health tool built with AI assumes the same user: fast internet, English as a...

item.title: Why Blocking Prompt Injection Is Wrong — and What to Do Instead
item.description: Every security tool blocks. Firewalls block. WAFs block. And now AI security tools block prompt injections too. But blocking is the wrong move — and here's why. The problem with blocking When your...

item.title: The AI code tools Dutch developers actually use in 2026 (field notes)
item.description: I run a Dutch AI-tools directory, and the question I get most from local developers is simple: which AI coding tool is worth paying for in 2026? Here are the field notes after watching what teams...

item.title: Samsung’s memory chip employees negotiated $340,000 bonuses this year
item.description: Details have emerged about a tentative deal struck between Samsung and semiconductor employees who had threatened to strike. The deal reportedly makes some workers eligible for average annual...

item.title: Automatic Error Recovery in AI Agent Networks
item.description: In a single-agent system, failure is simple: the agent errors, you retry. In multi-agent systems, failure is a graph problem. The Cascade Failure Problem Agent A: ✅ Success Agent B: ❌ Timeout...

item.title: You Are Not Choosing
item.description: You woke up this morning and checked your phone. Before your first cup of tea had brewed, you had already been nudged, filtered, ranked, and sorted by artificial intelligence dozens of times. The...

item.title: Building a Cinematic Adaptive Learning Intelligence with Gemma 4, Gemini, and OpenAI(Powered by Gemma 4)

item.title: CLAUDE.md for Angular: 13 Rules That Make AI Write Idiomatic, Production-Ready Components
item.description: Angular is the most opinionated framework in the frontend ecosystem — strong conventions, strict TypeScript, modules, services, guards, pipes, and a CLI that generates everything. But Claude Code...

item.title: I tested 7 vector databases for my RAG stack in 2026, here's the one nobody is talking about (yet)
item.description: TL;DR: I spent 1 week benchmarking Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, pgvector, SurrealDB, and AionDB on a real production RAG workload. The one I expected the least from completely smoked the...

item.title: Microsoft’s consumer marketing chief to leave next year
item.description: Yusuf Mehdi, executive vice president and consumer chief marketing officer at Microsoft, is leaving the company after 35 years. Mehdi announced his departure in an internal memo on Thursday,...

item.title: Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description: During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the...

item.title: Microsoft is letting Office users remove an annoying Copilot button
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out Office app updates next week that allow users to disable a floating Copilot button. The button appeared in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint in recent weeks, and floats above...

item.title: The Enhanced Games fit right in with the rest of 2026’s longevity vibes
item.description: This Sunday, a group of 42 athletes will gather in Las Vegas to compete in a somewhat unusual sporting competition. Participants in the inaugural Enhanced Games are being encouraged to take...

item.title: The Gulf’s AI Boom Has an Undersea Cable Problem
item.description: Hyperscalers are pushing the Gulf to rethink internet infrastructure as AI raises the stakes of cable disruptions.

item.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf. In...

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV)...

item.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
item.description: Cisco has rolled out updates for a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Secure Workload that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data. Tracked as CVE-2026-20223...

item.title: Can OpenAI’s ‘Master of Disaster’ Fix AI’s Reputation Crisis?
item.description: Global affairs chief Chris Lehane wants to tone down the debate over AI’s societal impacts—and get states to pass laws that won’t derail OpenAI’s meteoric rise.

item.title: Anker’s new earbuds are the first with its AI chip that boosts noise reduction
item.description: Anker has announced a new version of its Soundcore Liberty Pro earbuds: the new Liberty 5 Pro. These are the company's first earbuds featuring the Thus AI audio chip Anker announced last month,...

item.title: Anker’s new earbuds have the best call quality I’ve ever heard
item.description: Soundcore, Anker's audio brand, has mostly lived in the budget-to-midrange world, but with its new Liberty 5 Pro earbuds, it's aiming at the big guys. The two new earbuds - the Liberty 5 Pro and...

item.title: States ask judge to break up Live Nation-Ticketmaster
item.description: A federal judge is officially being tasked with deciding whether to break up Live Nation-Ticketmaster. More than 30 states are asking Judge Arun Subramanian to order a sale of the ticketing giant,...

item.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
item.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for...

item.title: Meta Is in Crisis, Google Search’s Makeover, and AI Gets Booed by Graduates
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we unpack the mass layoffs at Meta, big announcements at Google I/O, and the latest backlash against AI.

item.title: Roundtables: Can AI Learn to Understand the World?
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below AI companies want to build systems that understand the external world and overcome the limitations of LLMs. Recent developments have brought world models to...

item.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Firefox is working on a rounded redesign with easy-to-find controls for privacy and AI
item.description: Firefox is getting a drastic visual overhaul with a redesigned Settings section that will make it easier to find and use privacy settings, including the switch for turning off all present and...

item.title: In desperate times, graduates find hope in humiliating tech CEOs
item.description: University graduates are booing and heckling corporate executives who praise AI during their commencement ceremonies, and the only people who seem to be genuinely surprised by this are the...

item.title: Who gets to own the Luigi Mangione story?
item.description: On Monday morning, a judge overseeing the New York state case on the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO ruled that some evidence collected by police could not be shown to a jury. It wasn't the...
